title: Anestezi Programı - Ders İçeriği
content:
I. DÖNEM

2075G1001

Adjectives; Comparative adjectives, Superlative adjectives , Verb patterns love, enjoy, hate… ,Past Continuous Tense – when, while ,–for something which continued before and after another action, –for something that happened before and after a particular time, Past Simple vs Past Continuous , Simple Future Tense – Will ,- Making spontaneous decisions, - Future facts, - Predictions, Advice : Should / Should not (Shouldn’t), Present Perfect Tense, experiences, to connect the past to the present, for something that started in the past and continues in the present, Ever, never, just, already, yet, Been (when someone has gone to a place and returned) or Gone (when someone has not returned), Present Perfect vs Past Simple, If clause Type  Zero : when talking about our habits, our typical behaviors, explanations, guidelines, advices, orders and instructions, general truths, Real World and scientific facts, If clause Type I - a possible condition and its probable result, Modals can also be used in the main clause instead of the future tense to express the degree of certainty, permission, or a recommendation about the outcome, Going to - Future Tense / too - enough /one - ones / Compounds of some, any, no, every, Reported Commands and Requests, Revision

1011G1002

Anatomi (2-0-2/4)

1011G1003

Fizyoloji (2-0-2/4)

1011G1001

Tıbbi Terminoloji (2-0-2/3)

II. DÖNEM

2075B1001

2075B1002

2075B1003

Greetings & goodbyes ,The basic language for teaching structure – noun, verb, auxiliary verb, adjective, adverb, article, preposition, pronoun, ...,Subject Pronouns / Possessive adjectives / Possessive case,Singular & Plural nouns,determiners - This, that, these, those,Verb to be ( am / is / are ),Countries, nationalities &  languages,Simple Present Tense – Have, has ,Simple Present Tense – routine actions– permanent states– repeated actions and habits, adverbs of frequency– question forms such as Do you….. / How often do you …?,There is & There are / Quantifiers in questions and statements and with countable  & uncountable nouns ,(some, any, a lot of, lots of, many, much, a few, a little ) ,Imperatives / Days / Months / Seasons / Weather conditions,Present Continuous Tense ,– Actions / situations happening at the moment of speaking,– Comparison with Present Simple,Can / can’t - talking about ability and impossibility,Adjectives and Adverbs,Verb to be (was, were ),Simple Past Tense (Regular verbs),Simple Past Tense (Irregular verbs),– Questions with When, What, ….?,– Use of ‘ago’,– Past time expressions – yesterday, last week, etc.,Must - Mustn’t  for obligation and negative obligation,Have to - Has to for obligation and absence of obligation, Revision
